name: ZORVIX Research Assistant
description: Build structured threat research briefs, investigation questions, and next-step plans from a rough security prompt.
detail: Turn a loose cybersecurity question into a scoped research brief with follow-up prompts and actionable next steps.
prompt: Build a focused cybersecurity research brief for this topic and include the safest next steps.

name: Code Analyzer
description: Review code paths, surface likely security flaws, and turn findings into a remediation workflow.
detail: Use this workspace to prepare code-review prompts, attach artifacts to Zorvix, and launch guided security labs.
prompt: Analyze this code for security weaknesses, explain impact, and propose the safest remediation path.

name: Cyber Knowledge Tool
description: Search curated cybersecurity resources, defensive references, and learning material from one workspace.
detail: Use the knowledge workspace to surface trusted resources before you investigate, fix, or explain a concept.
prompt: Find the most relevant cybersecurity resources for this question and summarize the safest learning path.

name: Vulnerability Scanner
description: Organize vulnerability assessment work, triage findings, and convert scans into defensive action plans.
detail: This workspace helps you scope a vulnerability review, prepare Zorvix prompts, and move into guided remediation.
prompt: Help me triage this vulnerability finding, rate the risk, and plan the safest remediation sequence.

name: Endpoint Security Analyzer
description: Review endpoint exposure, suspicious behavior, and hardening priorities through a focused defensive workflow.
detail: Use this workspace to investigate endpoint issues, frame the right Zorvix question, and continue in lab mode.
prompt: Analyze this endpoint security issue, identify likely causes, and outline the safest containment steps.

name: Server Security Scanner
description: Map server-side security weaknesses, configuration risks, and response priorities in a structured workspace.
detail: Prepare a server security assessment with focused prompts, practical checks, and clean handoff into labs.
prompt: Review this server security situation, list the highest-risk gaps, and prioritize the remediation order.

name: Free Web Security Scanner
description: Run a professional web security scan for HTTPS enforcement, SSL health, header hardening, response time, and technology signals.
detail: Perform a structured web security check that highlights HTTPS usage, certificate health, redirect posture, security headers, and server hints.
prompt: Scan my website for SSL, HTTPS enforcement, response time, and basic server details.

name: OSINT Intelligence Tool
description: Create OSINT investigation plans, targeted research prompts, and evidence-collection checklists.
detail: Use this workspace to frame OSINT questions clearly before moving into domain, search, or evidence review tasks.
prompt: Design an OSINT investigation plan for this target and list the safest evidence collection steps.

name: Domain Investigation Tool
description: Inspect domain exposure, response headers, and verified attack-surface signals in one place.
detail: Use the domain workspace to analyze headers, review verified exposure signals, and prepare follow-up questions for Zorvix.
prompt: Investigate this domain, explain the main exposure points, and suggest the safest next defensive checks.

name: Google Dork Generator
description: Generate defensive Google dork queries for authorized research, exposure discovery, and awareness exercises.
detail: This workspace turns a target and category into a structured search query with explanation and defensive context.
prompt: Generate a defensive Google dork pattern for this authorized target and explain what it reveals.

name: Cyber Lab Workspace
description: Launch guided cybersecurity exercises with structured learning goals, checkpoints, and practical scenarios.
detail: Use this workspace to move from explanation to hands-on practice inside the Zero Day Guardian learning flow.
prompt: Create a hands-on cyber lab plan for this topic with a safe progression from beginner to advanced.

name: Practice Environment
description: Open a focused practice flow for repeating core cybersecurity skills and defensive procedures.
detail: Use the practice environment to keep repetition organized and translate concepts into repeatable operational steps.
prompt: Set up a repeatable practice environment for this cybersecurity skill and define the success checkpoints.

name: ZORVIX Learning Mentor
description: Build adaptive learning prompts, next-step exercises, and training tracks for any cybersecurity topic.
detail: Use this workspace to generate tailored learning plans, browse beginner labs, and route guided questions into Zorvix.
prompt: Teach this cybersecurity topic step by step and give me the next practical exercise to complete.
